fields.description:
## Context
Discovered while implementing epic sonic-columned-sturgeon (op-certs). Children 4214 (merge-gate
verify), ee0b (trusted-server producer), and 8f1d (HMAC‚Üíopcert migration) all depend on a shared
primitive: **how an asymmetric op-cert (a DSSE envelope) is stored on a ticket and produced.** The
epic decomposition treated this as implicit; it is a core event-sourcing change, so it gets its
own ticket.

Today attestations are HMAC records. `reducer/_processors.py::process_signature` builds the
`attestations[kind]` record from a FIXED field set (manifest/algorithm/signature/key_id/head_sha/
signed_at/author/kind) ‚Äî it does NOT preserve an added `envelope`. Storing an op-cert DSSE envelope
therefore requires a reducer change (runs on every ticket replay; the SNAPSHOT/compaction fold at
`_processors.py:763` restores `attestations` verbatim, so op-cert fields survive compaction for
free) plus a producer in `signing.py`.

## Selected design ‚Äî Option A (DECIDED 2026-07-13 by Joe Oakhart; no further confirmation needed)
Extend the existing `SIGNATURE` event record ‚Äî do NOT introduce a new event type. The *structural*
choice (carry the new signature inside the existing event/record rather than a new event type)
mirrors git signed commits (a `gpgsig` header on the existing commit object) and additive
Protobuf/Avro schema evolution.

**Cross-version behavior (accurate ‚Äî NOT tolerant-reader-transparent).** An op-cert `SIGNATURE`
event carries the DSSE `envelope` and OMITS the HMAC `signature`/`algorithm`. So an OLD clone does
not "ignore an unknown field and keep verifying": it reduces the event into a record with no HMAC
and reads it as **UNSIGNED** ‚Äî it cannot verify an op-cert because it lacks the asymmetric verifier.
This is exactly the identity epic's SSHSIG-authorship rollout posture (older clones preserve the
event append-only but cannot verify it) and the `TAG_DELTA` precedent: **op-cert events are
preserved-but-unverifiable on pre-upgrade clones; upgrade the verify/reconcile hosts first.** What
IS additive-transparent is the record *schema*: the new `envelope`/bound fields are present-only, so
legacy HMAC `sign_manifest` records are byte-unchanged and existing HMAC verification is untouched.

Concretely:

- **Producer** (`signing.sign_opcert_manifest`, parallel to `sign_manifest`): builds a DSSE
  envelope via `opcert.sign_opcert(ticket_id, material_fingerprint, merged_log_commit,
  key_path=‚Ä¶, principal=‚Ä¶)`, then appends a `SIGNATURE` event whose record carries the signed
  `manifest` (first line `"<kind>: ‚Ä¶"` so the reducer derives the kind), the encoded `envelope`
  (`dsse.encode`), the bound `material_fingerprint` + `merged_log_commit`, and `algorithm="sshsig"`
  ‚Äî NO HMAC signature.
- **Reducer** (`process_signature`): additionally preserve `envelope`, `material_fingerprint`,
  `merged_log_commit` into the `attestations[kind]` record (read via `data.get(...)`, present-only).
  No change needed to the compaction fold (it restores `attestations` verbatim).
- **Read helper** (`opcert.opcert_from_record`): returns `(dsse.Envelope, {material_fingerprint,
  merged_log_commit})` when the record carries an `envelope`, else `None` (distinguishes an op-cert
  record from a legacy HMAC record).
- **Rollout:** op-cert events are preserved append-only by older clones but read as UNSIGNED
  there (no asymmetric verifier) ‚Äî upgrade verify/reconcile hosts first, exactly as the SSHSIG
  authorship rollout and `TAG_DELTA` require. The record-schema extension itself is additive
  (legacy HMAC records unchanged).

(Considered and rejected: Option B ‚Äî a new `OPCERT` event type + separate `opcerts` map ‚Äî cleaner
separation but larger change and its separation is moot once 8f1d removes HMAC.)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `signing.sign_opcert_manifest(...)` writes an envelope-bearing `SIGNATURE` event; a test
      asserts the ticket's reduced `attestations[<kind>]` record then carries the encoded
      `envelope` + bound `material_fingerprint`/`merged_log_commit`, and NO HMAC `signature`.
- [ ] `process_signature` preserves those fields through a full reduce AND through a
      compact-then-replay round-trip; a test asserts an op-cert survives compaction unchanged.
- [ ] `opcert.opcert_from_record(record)` returns `(envelope, bound_fields)` for an op-cert record
      and `None` for a legacy HMAC record (test both); the returned envelope, fed to
      `opcert.verify_opcert` with a pinned keyring, certifies (end-to-end test).
- [ ] The record-schema extension is additive (present-only): a test asserts a legacy HMAC
      `sign_manifest` record is byte-unchanged (no `envelope`/bound keys) and still HMAC-verifies,
      so existing HMAC attestations are untouched. Rollout note documented: op-cert events are
      preserved-but-unverifiable on pre-upgrade clones (read as unsigned) ‚Äî upgrade verify/reconcile
      hosts first (as with SSHSIG authorship / `TAG_DELTA`).

## Success Criteria
- 4214 can read a stored op-cert to verify it over the merged log; ee0b/8f1d can produce op-certs
  onto tickets via `sign_opcert_manifest`.
